top_categories.workout_context: Perth Hills Gran Fondo's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Perth Hills Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
Perth Hills Gran Fondo's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 87 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Perth Hills Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
At Perth Hills Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
At mile 60 of 87, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 87 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Perth Hills Gran Fondo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Perth Hills Gran Fondo's mixed terrain actually does to you.
Tempo is Perth Hills Gran Fondo's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 87 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Perth Hills Gran Fondo’s 87 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Perth Hills Gran Fondo requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, G Spot, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 87 miles of mixed terrain in Kalamunda, Perth, Western Australia.
